definition: maximum value of the sum of delay time, slope time and ripple time of a voltage amplifier under small-signal conditions and compensated for unity gain at reference conditions

definition_ml.en: maximum value of the sum of delay time, slope time and ripple time of a voltage amplifier under small-signal conditions and compensated for unity gain at reference conditions

remark: The ripple time is the time interval between the end of the slope time and that instance at which the magnitude of the output signal reaches, for the last time, a specified level range containing the final output signal level. This level range is called ripple tolerance, and generally is 0.1% of the final output signal.

remark_ml.en: The ripple time is the time interval between the end of the slope time and that instance at which the magnitude of the output signal reaches, for the last time, a specified level range containing the final output signal level. This level range is called ripple tolerance, and generally is 0.1% of the final output signal.
